sections.paragraph:
ἀκωλύτως adv. (Pla. [Cra. 415d] +; freq. in pap as legal t.t.: POxy 502; BGU 917, 14; PLips 26, 11; 30, 9; Job 34:31 Sym.; TestAbr B 9 p. 113, 21 [Stone p. 74]; TestJob 45:4; JosAs 14:12 cod. A [p. 60, 2 Bat.]; Jos., Ant. 12, 104; 16, 41 ἀ. τὴν πάτριον εὐσέβειαν διαφυλάττειν) without hindrance διδάσκειν ἀ. Ac 28:31 (GDelling NovT. 15, ’73, 196–204).—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PLips PLips = Griech. Urkunden der Papyrussammlung zu Leipzig—List 4

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

NovT NovT = Novum Testamentum (periodical)—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 40
